---
title: Fiction Writing Inspired Injection Attacks in LLMs
authors:
  - name: Ana Clara Zoppi Serpa
    affiliation: UNICAMP - Institute of Computing - Recod.ai
    roles: writing
    corresponding: true
---

## Literature Review: Open Problems and Literature Gaps

### [@ChenPS025_StruQ]

**Paper summary**:

- Attacks rely on LLM ability to follow instructions + inability to separate prompts from user data
- Structured queries: general approach to solve the problem. Components:
    - (1) Secure front-end
    - (2) Specially trained LLM -> *structured instruction tuned LLM*
- Code available in the paper! :)
- Training process:
    - Augment standard instruction tuning dataset so that examples have instructions in the **data** part, train the LLM to ignore these instructions as they are not in the **prompt** part.
    - Train new LLM entirely from scratch - infeasible. Structured instruction tuning can be applied to **base LLMs**, so it's not from scratch, it's a new form of instruction tuning.
- Special separators, reserved tokens, front-end filters out these tokens from user prompt to avoid faking them
- Initialize embedding for the special token before tuning is important to keep model performance

**Suggested future work**:

- StruQ decreases ASR of manual attacks tpo 0% but some optimized attacks (TAP, GCG) still have high ASR
- StruQ is trained on task-agnostic manual injections, doesn't fully generalize to task-specific injections
    - Possible solution: craft task-dependent injections dataset with LLMs or human crowdsourcing
- StruQ protects programmatic applications that use API/libraries, but web chat-bot users wouldn't like manually marking their inputs as prompt x data constantly
- Prompt injection focus: StruQ doesn't prevent jailbreak, data extraction or other attacks, just prompt injection

### [@GongLZRCC0L25_Papillon]

**Summary**:

- Automated black-box jailbreaking framework inspired by SWE fuzzy testing concepts
- Proprietary APIs e.g. Gemini, GPT
- Code available
- Differential: 
    - This framework can start the jailbreaking process from an empty seed, no need for starter templates
    - Aims at producing natural, semantically coherent prompts, to bypass detection
    - Aims to shorten prompts -> attacks get cheaper because proprietary APIs charge on a token basis
- White-box jailbreaking x black-box jailbreaking distinction
- White-box well-known attack: GCG
- Black-box subdivides into handcrafted and automatic
- Jailbreaking defense types
    - Input modification
    - Prompt engineering
    - Output filtering
- Defenses Papillon breaks
    - perplexity
    - SmoothLLM
    - LlamaGuard

**Suggested future work**:

- Add more mutators to the framework for prompt evolution
- Evaluate different prompt compression methods
- Develop defenses
- Test Papillon on non-English LLM

### [@PasquiniKA25_LLMmap]

**Summary**:

- Fingerprinting LLM = figuring out what LLM is being used by an app -> analogous to OS fingerprinting
- How it works
    - Send carefully crafted queries to the app
    - Analyze responses
    - Identify specific LLM version in use
- 42 different LLM versions 95% accuracy just 8 interactions
- Why is it an attack?
    - Fingerprinting enables other attack surfaces, it's related to the **reconnaissance** stage of an attack
    - Knowing the model enables trying target adversarial prompts, vulnerabilities specific to that model etc
- Tool for AI red teams
- Code available
- Queries that trigger unique response patterns that might identify what LLM is responding
    - Meta information ("what is your version?")
    - Banner grabbing ("what is your name?")
    - Malformed
    - Alignment / harmful / controversial
    - Nonsensical, semantically broken
- Not necessarily the model answers truthfully to a banner grabbing, but it might have a *unique way to deflect the question*
- Collect traces from the queries & responses
- Use an ML inference model (handle diverse/variable responses)
- Open-set x closed-set
    - Closed-set: classifier
    - Open-set: siamese-like, contrastive learning ("is this LLM the same as this other LLM?")
- Prompt configuration universe -> ways LLMs get customized and become unique for a certain app
    - Hyperparameters (temperature, frequency penalty)
    - System prompt
    - Framework (RAG, COT, other...)
- Some defenses do reduce the accuracy of fingerprinting, but they have disadvantages, such as impacting model utility
- Findings suggest fingerprinting is an inevitable consequence of unique behavior exhibited by different models, unlike OS fingerprinting, which is avoidable with some measures

**Suggested future work**:

- GlitchTokens
- Automated query generation with optimization heuristics
- Additional inference - not only model + version, but also
    - tool call list
    - RAG / other internal framework being used
    - hyperparameters

### [@BiscontiPRGS26_AdversarialPoetry]

\textcolor{red}{to-do - lido no tablet}

### [@BiscontiGPSSN26_AdversarialTales]

\textcolor{red}{to-do - lido no tablet}

### [@IBM2025_PromptInjection]

A IBM web page explaining the basics of prompt injection - what it is, how it generally works, risks etc. Not too technical, not a research paper with experimental results.

### [@Alber2025_MedicalLLMsAreVulnerableToDataPoisoningAttacks]

\textcolor{red}{to-do - tem detalhes nos slides que apresentei no Horus}

### [@Youtube2024_SomeoneWon50kByMakingAIHallucinate],[@IlPost2024_Freysa]

Video and news article about someone winning $50k by making AI hallucinate on the Freysa competition.

### [@HeidingKLKS24_LLMPhishing]

\textcolor{red}{não cheguei a ler, tava na lista antiga do google docs}

### [@Kiela2021_TheHatefulMemesChallengeDetectingHateSpeechInMultimodalMemes]

\textcolor{red}{acho que eu li sim, deve estar no Drive ou no tablet, só não lembro os detalhes agora}

### [@Fersini2022_SemEval2022Task5MultimediaAutomaticMisogynyIdentification]

\textcolor{red}{n li ainda}

### [@Hwang2023_MemeCapCaptioningAndInterpretingMemes]

\textcolor{red}{n li ainda}

### [@Cardenuto2023_TheAgeOfSyntheticRealitiesChallengesAndOpportunities]

\textcolor{red}{li sim, é o paper do Anderson e do João, é um geralzão de tudo que tem rolado de realidade sintética}

### [@Farid2016_PhotoForensics]

\textcolor{red}{n li, é o livro de forense digital que fala das técnicas pré-LLM, Anderson tinha recomendado}

### [@Guo2025_DarkMindLatentChainOfThoughtBackdoor]

\textcolor{red}{to-do - li sim, tem detalhes em slide e no google docs, dá pra pegar}

### Some papers about KG + LLM, didn't read

[@Ibrahim2026], [@khorashadizadeh2024researchtrendsinterplaylarge], [@dai2025largelanguagemodelsbetter], [@kau2024combiningknowledgegraphslarge], [@Pan2024_UnifyingLargeLanguageModelsAndKnowledgeGraphsARoadmap]

### [@Ng2017_DeepLearningSpecialization]

\textcolor{red}{Curso de Deep Learning, relevante, gastei um tempão fazendo pra me especializar}

### Papers about anxiety in LLMs

[@codaforno2024inducinganxietylargelanguage]
[@Ben-Zion2025]

### Milestone papers about LLMs

These are really cool to see the historical trajectory, taken from this repository with several LLM resources and papers: [@Hannibal046AwesomeLLM]

- [@vaswani2023attentionneed] - Attention Is All You Need
- [@Radford2018ImprovingLU] - Improving Language Understanding by Generative Pre-Training
- [@devlin2019bertpretrainingdeepbidirectional] - BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding
- [@Radford2019LanguageMA] - Language Models are Unsupervised Multitask Learners
- [@shoeybi2020megatronlmtrainingmultibillionparameter] - Megatron-LM: Training Multi-Billion Parameter Language Models Using Model Parallelism
- [@JMLR:v21:20-074] - Exploring the Limits of Transfer Learning with a Unified Text-to-Text Transformer
- [@rajbhandari2020zeromemoryoptimizationstraining] - ZeRO: Memory Optimizations Toward Training Trillion Parameter Models
- [@kaplan2020scalinglawsneurallanguage] - Scaling Laws for Neural Language Models
- [@brown2020languagemodelsfewshotlearners] - Language Models are Few-Shot Learners
- [@fedus2022switchtransformersscalingtrillion] - Switch Transformers: Scaling to Trillion Parameter Models with Simple and Efficient Sparsity
- [@chen2021evaluatinglargelanguagemodels] - Evaluating Large Language Models Trained on Code
- [@bommasani2022opportunitiesrisksfoundationmodels] - On the Opportunities and Risks of Foundation Models
- [@wei2022finetunedlanguagemodelszeroshot] - Finetuned Language Models Are Zero-Shot Learners
- [@sanh2022multitaskpromptedtrainingenables] - Multitask Prompted Training Enables Zero-Shot Task Generalization
- [@du2022glamefficientscalinglanguage] - GLaM: Efficient Scaling of Language Models with Mixture-of-Experts
- [@Nakano2021WebGPTBQ] - WebGPT: Browser-assisted question-answering with human feedback
- [@borgeaud2022improvinglanguagemodelsretrieving] - Improving language models by retrieving from trillions of tokens
- [@rae2022scalinglanguagemodelsmethods] - Scaling Language Models: Methods, Analysis & Insights from Training Gopher
- [@wei2023chainofthoughtpromptingelicitsreasoning] - Chain-of-Thought Prompting Elicits Reasoning in Large Language Models
- [@thoppilan2022lamdalanguagemodelsdialog] - LaMDA: Language Models for Dialog Applications
- [@lewkowycz2022solvingquantitativereasoningproblems] - Solving Quantitative Reasoning Problems with Language Models
- [@smith2022usingdeepspeedmegatrontrain] - Using DeepSpeed and Megatron to Train Megatron-Turing NLG 530B, A Large-Scale Generative Language Model
- [@ouyang2022traininglanguagemodelsfollow] - Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback
- [@chowdhery2022palmscalinglanguagemodeling] - PaLM: Scaling Language Modeling with Pathways
- [@hoffmann2022trainingcomputeoptimallargelanguage] - Training Compute-Optimal Large Language Models
- [@zhang2022optopenpretrainedtransformer] - OPT: Open Pre-trained Transformer Language Models
- [@tay2023ul2unifyinglanguagelearning] - UL2: Unifying Language Learning Paradigms
- [@wei2022emergentabilitieslargelanguage] - Emergent Abilities of Large Language Models
- [@srivastava2023beyond] - Beyond the Imitation Game: Quantifying and extrapolating the capabilities of language models
- [@hao2022languagemodelsgeneralpurposeinterfaces] - Language Models are General-Purpose Interfaces
- [@glaese2022improvingalignmentdialogueagents] - Improving alignment of dialogue agents via targeted human judgements
- [@chung2022scalinginstructionfinetunedlanguagemodels] - Scaling Instruction-Finetuned Language Models
- [@zeng2023glm130bopenbilingualpretrained] - GLM-130B: An Open Bilingual Pre-trained Model
- [@liang2023holisticevaluationlanguagemodels] - Holistic Evaluation of Language Models
- [@workshop2023bloom176bparameteropenaccessmultilingual] - BLOOM: A 176B-Parameter Open-Access Multilingual Language Model
- [@taylor2022galacticalargelanguagemodel] - Galactica: A Large Language Model for Science
- [@iyer2023optimlscalinglanguagemodel] - OPT-IML: Scaling Language Model Instruction Meta Learning through the Lens of Generalization
- [@longpre2023flancollectiondesigningdata] - The Flan Collection: Designing Data and Methods for Effective Instruction Tuning
- [@touvron2023llamaopenefficientfoundation] - LLaMA: Open and Efficient Foundation Language Models
- [@huang2023languageneedaligningperception] - Language Is Not All You Need: Aligning Perception with Language Models
- [@orvieto2023resurrectingrecurrentneuralnetworks] - Resurrecting Recurrent Neural Networks for Long Sequences
- [@driess2023palmeembodiedmultimodallanguage] - PaLM-E: An Embodied Multimodal Language Model
- [@openai2024gpt4technicalreport] - GPT-4 Technical Report
- [@liu2023visualinstructiontuning] - Visual Instruction Tuning
- [@biderman2023pythiasuiteanalyzinglarge] - Pythia: A Suite for Analyzing Large Language Models Across Training and Scaling
- [@sun2023principledrivenselfalignmentlanguagemodels] - Principle-Driven Self-Alignment of Language Models from Scratch with Minimal Human Supervision
- [@anil2023palm2technicalreport] - PaLM 2 Technical Report
- [@peng2023rwkvreinventingrnnstransformer] - RWKV: Reinventing RNNs for the Transformer Era
- [@rafailov2024directpreferenceoptimizationlanguage] - Direct Preference Optimization: Your Language Model is Secretly a Reward Model
- [@yao2023treethoughtsdeliberateproblem] - Tree of Thoughts: Deliberate Problem Solving with Large Language Models
- [@touvron2023llama2openfoundation] - Llama 2: Open Foundation and Fine-Tuned Chat Models
- [@jiang2023mistral7b] - Mistral 7B
- [@gu2024mambalineartimesequencemodeling] - Mamba: Linear-Time Sequence Modeling with Selective State Spaces
- [@deepseekai2024deepseekv2strongeconomicalefficient] - DeepSeek-V2: A Strong, Economical, and Efficient Mixture-of-Experts Language Model
- [@groeneveld-etal-2024-olmo] - OLMo: Accelerating the Science of Language Models
- [@dao2024transformersssmsgeneralizedmodels] - Transformers are SSMs: Generalized Models and Efficient Algorithms Through Structured State Space Duality
- [@grattafiori2024llama3herdmodels] - The Llama 3 Herd of Models
- [@penedo2024finewebdatasetsdecantingweb] - The FineWeb Datasets: Decanting the Web for the Finest Text Data at Scale
- [@muennighoff2025olmoeopenmixtureofexpertslanguage] - OLMoE: Open Mixture-of-Experts Language Models
- [@qwen2025qwen25technicalreport] - Qwen2.5 Technical Report
- [@deepseekai2025deepseekv3technicalreport] - DeepSeek-V3 Technical Report
- [@Guo_2025] - DeepSeek-R1 incentivizes reasoning in LLMs through reinforcement learning

### Google Research

- [@52868] - InstructPipe: Generating Visual Blocks Pipelines with Human Instructions and LLMs
- [@1021008] - Gemini \& Physical World: Large Language Models Can Estimate the Intensity of Earthquake Shaking from Multi-Modal Social Media Posts
- [@1025553] - SSDTrain: Faster Large Language Model Training Using SSD-Based Activation Offloading
- [@1024745] - Triaging mammography with artificial intelligence: an implementation study
- [@1024143] - Mufu: Multilingual Fused Learning for Low-Resource Translation with LLM
- [@1026765] - TOKENFORMER: Rethinking Transformers Scaling with Tokenized Model Parameters
- [@morris2024generativeghostsanticipatingbenefits] - Generative Ghosts: Anticipating Benefits and Risks of AI Afterlives
- [@lee2025geminiembeddinggeneralizableembeddings] - Gemini Embedding: Generalizable Embeddings from Gemini
- [@maninis2025tipstextimagepretrainingspatial] - TIPS: Text-Image Pretraining with Spatial awareness
- [@wang2025scalingpretrainingbilliondata] - Scaling Pre-training to One Hundred Billion Data for Vision Language Models
- [@cooper2025machineunlearningdoesntthink] - Machine Unlearning Doesn't Do What You Think: Lessons for Generative AI Policy and Research
- [@schultz2025masteringboardgamesexternal] - Mastering Board Games by External and Internal Planning with Language Models
- [@dambrosio2025achievinghumanlevelcompetitive] - Achieving Human Level Competitive Robot Table Tennis
- [@Hutter:24uaibook2] - An Introduction to Universal Artificial Intelligence
- [@panda2024teach] - Teach LLMs to Phish: Stealing Private Information from Language Models
- [@whitney2024learning] - Learning 3D Particle-based Simulators from RGB-D Videos
- [@song2024positionleveragefoundationalmodels] - Position: Leverage Foundational Models for Black-Box Optimization

### Microsoft Research

- [@vorvoreanu2025fostering] - Fostering appropriate reliance on GenAI: Lessons learned from early research
- [@flores-saviaga2025the] - The Impact of Generative AI Coding Assistants on Developers Who Are Visually Impaired
- [@alshammari2025a] - A Unifying Framework for Representation Learning

### OpenAI

- [@miserendino2025swelancerfrontierllmsearn] - SWE-Lancer: Can Frontier LLMs Earn \$1 Million from Real-World Freelance Software Engineering?
- [@baker2022videopretrainingvptlearning] - Video PreTraining (VPT): Learning to Act by Watching Unlabeled Online Videos
- [@radford2021learningtransferablevisualmodels] - Learning Transferable Visual Models From Natural Language Supervision (CLIP)
- [@openai2021dalle] - DALL·E: Creating Images from Text
- [@openai2018montezumasrevenge] - Learning Montezuma's Revenge from a single demonstration
- [@openai2022languagemodelsafety] - Lessons learned on language model safety and misuse
- [@goh2021multimodal] - Multimodal Neurons in Artificial Neural Networks

### Synthetic Media Detection

- [@9141516] - FakeCatcher: Detection of Synthetic Portrait Videos using Biological Signals
- [@unknown] - Synthetic Realities in the Digital Age: Navigating the Opportunities and Challenges of AI-Generated Content
- [@ye2025lokicomprehensivesyntheticdata] - LOKI: A Comprehensive Synthetic Data Detection Benchmark using Large Multimodal Models
- [@Zobaed2021] - DeepFakes: Detecting Forged and Synthetic Media Content Using Machine Learning
- [@10095167] - On The Detection of Synthetic Images Generated by Diffusion Models
- [@10409290] - CIFAKE: Image Classification and Explainable Identification of AI-Generated Synthetic Images
- [@laurier2024catmousegameongoing] - The Cat and Mouse Game: The Ongoing Arms Race Between Diffusion Models and Detection Methods

### Ilya Sutskever's Recommended Reading List

Papers and resources from [@dzyim2023ilyasutskeverreading], originally shared by Ilya Sutskever with John Carmack in 2020.

- [@rush2018annotated] - The Annotated Transformer
- [@aaronson2011first] - The First Law of Complexodynamics
- [@karpathy2015unreasonable] - The Unreasonable Effectiveness of Recurrent Neural Networks
- [@olah2015understanding] - Understanding LSTM Networks
- [@zaremba2014recurrent] - Recurrent Neural Network Regularization
- [@hinton1993keeping] - Keeping the Neural Networks Simple by Minimizing the Description Length of the Weights
- [@vinyals2015pointer] - Pointer Networks
- [@krizhevsky2012imagenet] - ImageNet Classification with Deep Convolutional Neural Networks
- [@vinyals2016order] - Order Matters: Sequence to Sequence for Sets
- [@huang2019gpipe] - GPipe: Easy Scaling with Micro-Batch Pipeline Parallelism
- [@he2016deep] - Deep Residual Learning for Image Recognition
- [@yu2016multi] - Multi-Scale Context Aggregation by Dilated Convolutions
- [@gilmer2017neural] - Neural Message Passing for Quantum Chemistry
- [@bahdanau2016neural] - Neural Machine Translation by Jointly Learning to Align and Translate
- [@he2016identity] - Identity Mappings in Deep Residual Networks
- [@santoro2017simple] - A Simple Neural Network Module for Relational Reasoning
- [@chen2017variational] - Variational Lossy Autoencoder
- [@santoro2018relational] - Relational Recurrent Neural Networks
- [@aaronson2014quantifying] - Quantifying the Rise and Fall of Complexity in Closed Systems: The Coffee Automaton
- [@graves2014neural] - Neural Turing Machines
- [@amodei2016deep] - Deep Speech 2: End-to-End Speech Recognition in English and Mandarin
- [@grunwald2004tutorial] - A Tutorial Introduction to the Minimum Description Length Principle
- [@legg2008machine] - Machine Super Intelligence (PhD Thesis)
- [@shen2017kolmogorov] - Kolmogorov Complexity and Algorithmic Randomness
- [@feifei2016cs231n] - CS231n: Convolutional Neural Networks for Visual Recognition

### Misc, to be organized

- [@kumar-etal-2023-language] - Language Generation Models Can Cause Harm: So What Can We Do About It? An Actionable Survey
- [@agrawal2024knowledgegraphsreducehallucinations] - Can Knowledge Graphs Reduce Hallucinations in LLMs? : A Survey
- [@feng2023knowledgesolverteachingllms] - Knowledge Solver: Teaching LLMs to Search for Domain Knowledge from Knowledge Graphs
- [@lu2024aiscientistfullyautomated] - The AI Scientist: Towards Fully Automated Open-Ended Scientific Discovery
- [@doi:10.1126/science.adq1814] - Durably reducing conspiracy beliefs through dialogues with AI
- [@nakanishi2025scalablesoftmaxsuperiorattention] - Scalable-Softmax Is Superior for Attention
- [@feng2024rnnsneeded] - Were RNNs All We Needed?
- [@zhang2025thoughtactionhierarchyneural] - From Thought to Action: How a Hierarchy of Neural Dynamics Supports Language Production
- [@lévy2025braintotextdecodingnoninvasiveapproach] - Brain-to-Text Decoding: A Non-invasive Approach via Typing
- [@li-etal-2025-loki] - Loki: An Open-Source Tool for Fact Verification

### Creative writing research

- [@panda2025saydifferentlylinguisticstyles] - lido, anotei no caderno, achei legal que tem bastante exemplo das prompts.

- [@tang2024rolebreakcharacterhallucinationjailbreak] - RoleBreak: Character Hallucination as a Jailbreak Attack in Role-Playing Systems - não é sobre jailbreak do jeito que eu achava que seria, é só sobre fazer personagens se comportarem de forma inconsistente, não é sobre convencer o LLM a fazer output harmuful, apesar de ser muito citado em outros artigos de jailbreak... Vai entender...

- [@zhang2025enhancingjailbreakattacksllms] - Enhancing Jailbreak Attacks on LLMs via Persona Prompts - gostei bastante e tem repo do GitHub
